paper_title: Chironomid Taxa Analysis in Enol Lake

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze chironomid taxa distribution and environmental characteristics.

steps.description: The general distribution of the chironomid taxa along depth was represented by the program Psimpoll, using the mean of the relative abundances of each taxa per depth.
steps.searchable_summary: represent chironomid taxa distribution using Psimpoll

steps.description: Rare taxa, defined by those that did not reach a relative abundance of ≥2% in at least two samples, were removed from the analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out rare taxa with <2% abundance

steps.description: For the statistical analyses, we used total abundances, which were previously transformed to log (x+1).

Assemblage ordination was explored through Non-metric Multidimensional Scaling (NMDS) of the species matrix using the function 'metaMDS' of the R package 'vegan' and the Bray Curtis dissimilarity index.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS using 'metaMDS' and Bray Curtis index

steps.description: We classified the samples in 3 groups through a k-means clustering.
Indicator taxa of each group were identified through an IndVal analysis using the 'multipatt' function in the R package 'indicspecies'.
steps.searchable_summary: identify indicator taxa using IndVal analysis

steps.description: The differences in the environmental characteristics of each group were tested using ANOVA and Tukey tests for pairwise comparisons.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and Tukey tests on environmental characteristics

steps.description: Temporal variations in the chironomid assemblages were explored through a Redundancy Analysis (RDA) at 5 depths with forward selection of explanatory variables.
Spearman correlation matrices were calculated to discard variables that were significantly correlated (ρ≥0.85).

methods.description: Method for collecting chironomid samples and environmental data from Enol Lake.

steps.description: Samplings were performed in two consecutive years (2013 and 2014), in May, July, September and November of each year.
In every sampling campaign, both littoral and bottom samples were collected.
Samples were collected using the kick-sampling method with a 250 µm mesh net and preserved in formaldehyde at 4%.
In the sublittoral and profundal zones, an Ekman grab was used with three replicates per sample following a depth transect.
steps.searchable_summary: use Ekman grab for sublittoral and profundal sampling

steps.description: In the laboratory, Chironomidae larvae were sorted up to a maximum of 300 individuals and identified using an optical microscope.
Environmental variables were collected by researchers using a multiparametric sensor and water samples were analyzed in the laboratory.
